seoTitle: How to Design a Typeform That Converts
metaDescription: Learn how to customize themes, colors, fonts, and images in Typeform to create a professional, high-converting form design.
intro: A form's design affects how professional it looks and how likely people are to complete it. This guide covers how to design a Typeform by customizing themes, colors, fonts, layouts, and images, plus tips for keeping the design consistent across desktop and mobile.

steps.heading: Open the Design panel
steps.body: Access the design section of the Typeform editor where all visual customization options are located.

steps.heading: Customize theme and branding
steps.body: Adjust colors, fonts, and overall theme to reflect your brand or personal style.

steps.heading: Add images and visual elements
steps.body: Incorporate images, logos, and welcome screens to make the form more visually engaging.

steps.heading: Preview and publish your design
steps.body: Check how the form looks on both desktop and mobile before publishing to ensure a consistent experience.

faq.question: Does design affect form completion rates?
faq.answer: Yes, a clean, professional design tends to increase engagement and completion rates compared to a plain, unstyled form.

faq.question: Can I preview how my form looks on mobile before publishing?
faq.answer: Yes, Typeform lets you preview the form so you can check its appearance and layout across devices.

faq.question: What design elements can I customize in Typeform?
faq.answer: You can customize themes, colors, fonts, layouts, images, logos, and welcome screens.

faq.question: Do I need design skills to make a good-looking form?
faq.answer: No, Typeform's built-in design tools and themes make it possible to create an attractive form without advanced design experience.
